Full Responses

▶ #102 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ stop-long-phrase 1143 tokens · 119.6 tok/s

API variant: stop-long-phrase temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096 stop: In conclusion
Perf finish: stop load: 8.0s gen: 9.56s prompt_tok: 41 comp_tok: 1143 tok/s: 119.6
🎯 Test Intent
Tests stop on a long multi-word phrase. Feature: stop with multi-token string.
Expected: essay contains paragraphs 1-2 about renewable energy, stops before
"In conclusion" appears. Tests buffer-based stop detection across token boundaries.
PROMPT Write a 3-paragraph essay about renewable energy. Start the last paragraph with "In conclusion".

▶ #78 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ stop-double-newline 742 tokens · 99.6 tok/s

API variant: stop-double-newline temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096 stop:
Perf finish: stop load: 10.0s gen: 7.45s prompt_tok: 37 comp_tok: 742 tok/s: 99.6
🎯 Test Intent
Tests stop on double newline. Feature: stop parameter with "\n\n".
Expected: exactly one paragraph (about the ocean). The second paragraph about
mountains should NOT appear. finish_reason="stop".
PROMPT Write a short paragraph about the ocean. Then write a second paragraph about mountains.

▶ #161 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ tool-call-multi 1217 tokens · 97.2 tok/s

API variant: tool-call-multi temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 9.0s gen: 12.53s prompt_tok: 35 comp_tok: 1217 tok/s: 97.2
🎯 Test Intent
Tests multiple tool calls in one response. Feature: parallel tool calling.
Expected: TWO tool calls — get_weather(city="London") AND get_time(timezone="Asia/Tokyo").
finish_reason="tool_calls". Tests that the parser handles multiple <tool_call> blocks.
PROMPT What is the weather in London and what time is it in Tokyo?

▶ #136 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ think-normal 833 tokens · 96.7 tok/s

API variant: think-normal temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 9.0s gen: 8.62s prompt_tok: 49 comp_tok: 833 tok/s: 96.7
🎯 Test Intent
Tests think extraction on non-thinking model. Feature: <think> tag extraction.
Expected: answer is "150 miles" with step-by-step work. reasoning_content should
be empty/null — this model has no <think> support. If reasoning_content is populated,
the model is unexpectedly emitting <think> tags (possible chat template issue).
PROMPT Solve step by step: If a train travels at 60 mph for 2.5 hours, how far does it go?

▶ #130 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ response-format-json 1305 tokens · 96.6 tok/s

API variant: response-format-json temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096 resp_fmt: {"type":"json_object"}
Perf finish: stop json: valid load: 8.0s gen: 13.5s prompt_tok: 41 comp_tok: 1305 tok/s: 96.6
🎯 Test Intent
Tests json_object response format. Feature: response_format: json_object.
Expected: valid JSON with keys "language", "year_created", "creator" and values
"Python", 1991, "Guido van Rossum". Verify is_valid_json=true. Uses prompt injection
(not constrained decoding) — output may include markdown fences around JSON.
PROMPT Return a JSON object with keys "language", "year_created", and "creator" for Python.

▶ #108 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ stop-immediate 666 tokens · 96.6 tok/s

API variant: stop-immediate temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096 stop: The, I, A
Perf finish: stop load: 7.0s gen: 6.89s prompt_tok: 27 comp_tok: 666 tok/s: 96.6
🎯 Test Intent
Tests immediate stop (first token matches). Feature: stop on common first tokens.
Expected: empty or near-empty output. The model's first token is likely "The", "I",
or "A" — all are in the stop list. finish_reason="stop", completion_tokens near 0.
PROMPT Explain what gravity is.

▶ #170 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ tool-call-nullable-param 587 tokens · 96.4 tok/s

API variant: tool-call-nullable-param temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 8.0s gen: 6.09s prompt_tok: 28 comp_tok: 587 tok/s: 96.4
🎯 Test Intent
Tests tool call with nullable parameter schema (regression: PR #33).
Expected: get_weather tool call with location as a string. The "units" parameter has
anyOf [string, null] with default null — this must NOT crash Jinja template rendering.
finish_reason="tool_calls". Validates nullable schemas don't cause server errors.
PROMPT What is the weather in Chicago?

▶ #158 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ tool-call-xml 762 tokens · 96.1 tok/s

CLI afm: --tool-call-parser xmlFunction
API variant: tool-call-xml temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 9.0s gen: 7.93s prompt_tok: 31 comp_tok: 762 tok/s: 96.1
🎯 Test Intent
Tests explicit xmlFunction parser. Feature: --tool-call-parser xmlFunction.
Expected: get_weather tool call with city="Paris", unit="celsius".
Same as tool-call-auto but explicitly sets the parser — verifies the flag works.
PROMPT What is the weather in Paris in celsius?

▶ #138 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ think-raw 833 tokens · 96.0 tok/s

CLI afm: --raw
API variant: think-raw temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 9.0s gen: 8.68s prompt_tok: 49 comp_tok: 833 tok/s: 96.0
🎯 Test Intent
Tests raw mode on non-thinking model. Feature: --raw (skip think extraction).
Expected: same answer as think-normal (150 miles). Any <think> tags in output
pass through as literal text in content field. reasoning_content should be empty.
Verify no crash when --raw is used with a model that doesn't emit <think>.
PROMPT Solve step by step: If a train travels at 60 mph for 2.5 hours, how far does it go?

▶ #180 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ multilingual 1008 tokens · 95.9 tok/s

API variant: multilingual temp: 0.3 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 11.0s gen: 10.51s prompt_tok: 45 comp_tok: 1008 tok/s: 95.9
🎯 Test Intent
Tests multilingual output. Feature: tokenizer handling of diverse scripts.
Expected: correct translations in French (Bonjour), Spanish (Hola), Japanese (こんにちは),
and Arabic (مرحبا). Tests that tokenizer handles non-Latin scripts without garbling.
PROMPT Translate "Hello, how are you?" into French, Spanish, Japanese, and Arabic. Format as a numbered list.

▶ #145 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ max-completion-tokens 2355 tokens · 95.8 tok/s

API variant: max-completion-tokens temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 9.0s gen: 24.57s prompt_tok: 32 comp_tok: 2355 tok/s: 95.8
🎯 Test Intent
Tests max_completion_tokens alias. Feature: max_completion_tokens (OpenAI SDK field).
Expected: output truncated at ~100 tokens, finish_reason="length". This field is an
alias for max_tokens used by newer OpenAI SDK versions. Verifies the alias is parsed.
PROMPT Explain the causes and consequences of the French Revolution.

▶ #172 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ tool-call-none 470 tokens · 95.8 tok/s

API variant: tool-call-none temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 8.0s gen: 4.91s prompt_tok: 29 comp_tok: 470 tok/s: 95.8
🎯 Test Intent
Tests tool-bait prompt WITHOUT tools provided. Feature: no-tool hallucination check.
Expected: model answers naturally ("I don't have real-time data" or general info),
does NOT hallucinate tool_calls. finish_reason="stop" (not "tool_calls").
This is a weather question that would trigger tool calls if tools were available.
PROMPT What is the current temperature in Berlin?

▶ #155 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ tool-call-auto 449 tokens · 95.7 tok/s

API variant: tool-call-auto temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 10.0s gen: 4.69s prompt_tok: 28 comp_tok: 449 tok/s: 95.7
🎯 Test Intent
Tests auto-detected tool call format. Feature: tool calling with auto-detected parser.
Expected: model emits a get_weather tool call with city="Tokyo" (or similar).
finish_reason="tool_calls". The parser should auto-detect xmlFunction for Qwen models.
Verify tool_calls array in response contains valid function name and arguments.
PROMPT What is the weather in Tokyo?

▶ #82 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ stop-period 741 tokens · 95.6 tok/s

API variant: stop-period temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096 stop: .
Perf finish: stop load: 9.0s gen: 7.75s prompt_tok: 30 comp_tok: 741 tok/s: 95.6
🎯 Test Intent
Tests stop on punctuation (period). Feature: stop parameter with ".".
Expected: only the beginning of the first sentence, cut off before the first period.
finish_reason="stop". Very aggressive stop — output may be just a few words.
PROMPT Tell me about the sun in three sentences.

▶ #176 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ long-prompt 831 tokens · 95.3 tok/s

API variant: long-prompt temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 25.0s gen: 8.72s prompt_tok: 110 comp_tok: 831 tok/s: 95.3
🎯 Test Intent
Tests long repetitive prompt. Feature: prefill performance + context handling.
Expected: model repeats the sentence 8 times then says "DONE". Tests that long
prompts don't cause OOM, truncation, or garbled output during prefill.
PROMPT Repeat after me exactly: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. Now say DONE.

discount, subtotal, and tax steps correctly. Tests reasoning without <think> support.
PROMPT A store sells notebooks for $3.50 each. If you buy 5 or more, you get a 20% discount. Sales tax is 8.5%. How much do 7 notebooks cost after tax? Show your work.

Tests multiple stop sequences via API. Feature: stop parameter with array of 2 strings.
Expected: output should stop at whichever of "```" or "END" appears first in the
generated text. Likely stops at the closing ``` of the code block. finish_reason="stop".
PROMPT Name three primary colors and explain why they matter in design. Answer concisely.

but may slightly degrade quality. Compare output quality with small-kv.

than presence penalty (multiplicative vs additive). Output should stay coherent.

faster because the system prompt KV cache is reused from turn 1 (cache hit).
PROMPT Name three primary colors and explain why they matter in design. Answer concisely.
